novel_features.name: Portfolio performance tracker
novel_features.description: Track YTD / 1Y / all-time returns on your holdings with cost basis, dividends, and current price baked in.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining local cost-basis state with live quotes and historical dividends — no Yahoo endpoint provides this because cost basis only exists on the user's machine.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs to report 'how is my user's portfolio doing this year' — no single Yahoo Finance endpoint encodes cost basis.

novel_features.name: Morning digest across watchlist
novel_features.description: One command: overnight news + biggest movers + earnings today + ex-div dates for everything on your watchlist.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates data across quote, news, calendarEvents, and dailyGainers filtered by local watchlist — no single API call returns this view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as a single agent call for a 'daily market briefing' across a named set of tickers — replaces 4+ separate endpoint calls.

novel_features.name: Dividend income + yield-on-cost
novel_features.description: See total dividend income for the year, per-holding breakdown, and yield on cost.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local portfolio lots with cached dividend history. Yield-on-cost requires the original cost basis the user typed in.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user asks 'how much have I earned in dividends this year' or 'what's my real yield on my JNJ position'.

novel_features.name: Options moneyness + DTE filter
novel_features.description: Filter an options chain to ATM/OTM/ITM contracts within a days-to-expiration window.
novel_features.rationale: Yahoo's options endpoint returns the full chain; computing moneyness against spot and DTE requires local processing.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to surface 'OTM weekly puts on AAPL near earnings' without parsing a thousand-row chain.

novel_features.name: Local SQL screener over synced fundamentals
novel_features.description: Run arbitrary P/E, P/B, yield, margin, and growth filters against the data you've synced locally.
novel_features.rationale: Yahoo's remote screener is limited to 12 predefined IDs. Arbitrary filters require a local fundamentals index.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to compose custom value/growth screens an agent can apply to a synced universe, far beyond Yahoo's 12 canned screens.

novel_features.name: Chrome cookie import fallback
novel_features.description: Import your live Chrome session cookies when Yahoo's crumb handshake is blocked from your IP.
novel_features.rationale: Works around residential and cloud IP rate limits that kill raw API access. No other Yahoo Finance tool offers this fallback.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent's `doctor` reports 429 from the host's IP — Chrome's session cookies unblock the crumb handshake.

novel_features.name: Insider net-buying signal screener
novel_features.description: Surface companies where insiders are net buyers in the last N days, filtered to your watchlist.
novel_features.rationale: Joins insider transactions × watchlist membership and computes buy/sell ratios — needs local state.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs a 'who's insiders actually buying' signal layer across the user's watchlist.

novel_features.name: Total-return comparator
novel_features.description: Multi-symbol price-plus-dividend total return ranked over a range.
novel_features.rationale: Joins cached history × dividends to compute total return (price appreciation + reinvested dividends) — single-shot from local data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when comparing actual holding-period returns rather than price-only deltas — the only Yahoo CLI that does this offline.

novel_features.name: Covered-call screener over holdings
novel_features.description: Scan your holdings for covered-call candidates by annualized yield and DTE.
novel_features.rationale: Joins portfolio_lots (shares >=100) × options chains × spot quote. Computes annualized yield = (call_bid / spot) * (365/DTE).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to surface 'wheel-strategy' covered-call candidates from the user's actual stock positions.

novel_features.name: Watchlist correlation matrix
novel_features.description: Pairwise Pearson correlation across the symbols in a named watchlist over a date range.
novel_features.rationale: Pulls cached daily closes for every symbol in the watchlist, computes pairwise correlation locally — portfolio-theory diversification check.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to flag concentration risk inside a watchlist — 'is this 'tech' watchlist actually 80% AAPL exposure'.

narrative.headline: Every Yahoo Finance endpoint plus a SQLite portfolio, covered-call screener, and Chrome-cookie fallback the libraries don't ship.
narrative.value_prop: yahoo-finance-pp-cli matches yfinance and yahoo-finance2 for raw endpoint coverage, then adds the things only a local store + agent-shaped CLI can do: cost-basis-aware portfolio performance, dividend income with yield-on-cost, a SQL-backed fundamentals screener, options moneyness + DTE filtering, and an `auth login --chrome` cookie escape hatch when Yahoo blocks your IP.
narrative.auth_narrative: Yahoo Finance has no API key — it requires a crumb+cookie handshake. The CLI auto-fetches the crumb on first call and persists cookies to `~/.config/yahoo-finance-pp-cli/`. If your IP is rate-limited (cloud hosts and many international IPs are), run `auth login --chrome` to import a logged-in Chrome session so the crumb dance succeeds.

quickstart.comment: Confirms the crumb handshake works from your IP.
Pulls daily chart for AAPL; auto-caches into the local SQLite store.
Cross-endpoint daily briefing for a named watchlist — the killer transcendence command.

quickstart.command: yahoo-finance-pp-cli sql "SELECT symbol, MAX(close) FROM history GROUP BY symbol"
quickstart.comment: Compose raw SQL against synced data — uniqueness of the local store.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `yahoo-finance-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to import a logged-in Chrome session, then retry.

troubleshoots.symptom: Crumb appears in error responses but request still fails
troubleshoots.fix: Delete the cached session: `rm ~/.config/yahoo-finance-pp-cli/session.json` and let the next call re-bootstrap.
Free tier is delayed up to 15 minutes; `--extended-hours` does not change that. Use a paid provider for sub-minute data.
Seed your lots first: `yahoo-finance-pp-cli portfolio add AAPL 100 --purchase-date 2025-01-15 --cost-basis 175.00`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent needs Yahoo Finance market data with deterministic JSON output, a local store that compounds across calls (portfolio, watchlist, history cache), or a working session from a rate-limited host. It is the only Yahoo Finance tool that joins your cost basis with live data and survives a 429 via a Chrome cookie fallback.

recipes.title: Daily market briefing across your watchlist
recipes.explanation: Single command returns overnight news, biggest movers, earnings today, and ex-div dates for the symbols in the named watchlist.

recipes.title: Year-to-date portfolio performance
recipes.explanation: Joins cost basis with live quotes and reinvested dividends. The `--select` narrows the payload so an agent doesn't burn context on every row.

recipes.title: Find OTM puts on AAPL near 30 DTE
recipes.explanation: Filters the full chain client-side by moneyness and DTE; Yahoo's endpoint doesn't filter.

recipes.title: Custom SQL screen for cheap large caps
recipes.explanation: Runs against locally-synced fundamentals; Yahoo's remote screener has only 12 predefined IDs.

recipes.title: Raw SQL over the local store
recipes.command: yahoo-finance-pp-cli sql "SELECT symbol, ROUND(AVG(close), 2) AS avg_close FROM history WHERE date > date('now','-30 days') GROUP BY symbol ORDER BY avg_close DESC LIMIT 10"
recipes.explanation: Demonstrates the SQLite path; useful when an agent needs aggregations that no canned command covers.
